framework: - Cost-Sharing: Part A deductible, Part B 20% coinsurance, donut hole

ai_instructions.task: Produce a Medicare contracted providers report
framework: - Provider Network: count of participating providers, geographic distribution, network adequacy by service area
- Provider Enrollment: PECOS application, NPI, CMS-855 forms, Medicare Conditions of Participation, opt-out rules
- Quality Oversight: monitor service quality, fraud-and-abuse referrals, sanctions for documented violations
- Claims & Cost Review: audit Medicare fund use, identify outlier billing, flag medically unnecessary services
- Annual Performance Review & Termination: rate providers, publish star ratings where applicable, set rules for termination of provider agreements after sustained noncompliance

ai_instructions.task: Explain how to use medical care outside your home network or while traveling, and how to get reimbursed
framework: - When You're Covered: emergency care anywhere, urgent care while traveling, prior-authorization rules for non-emergency out-of-network visits
- Finding a Provider: how to use the plan's directory or call member services to find an in-network or covered provider out of area
- Payment at the Visit: when the provider bills the plan directly vs. when you pay upfront and submit a claim
- Filing for Reimbursement: which forms, supporting documents (itemized bill, medical records, proof of payment), submission deadlines, and expected processing time

ai_instructions.task: Produce a Social Security enrollment explainer
framework: - Coordination: spousal/survivor benefits, totalization agreements (foreign work)

ai_instructions.task: Produce a pension benefit estimator

ai_instructions.task: Produce a UI claim guide

ai_instructions.role: DOL Employment & Training Administration (ETA) reemployment policy lead
ai_instructions.task: Produce a reemployment support policy
Explain who qualifies for affordable-housing programs and how the application works
framework: - Programs Covered: Section 8 housing choice vouchers, public housing, LIHTC tax-credit properties — note who runs each
- Income Limits: Area Median Income (AMI) bands (e.g., 30% / 50% / 80% AMI), how household size adjusts the limit
- Household Definition: who counts in the household, how earned vs. unearned income is calculated
- Documents Needed: photo ID and Social Security cards for each member, recent pay stubs or tax returns, proof of current address
- Waitlist & Selection: how to apply with the local Public Housing Authority, typical wait times, lottery vs. preference points (veterans, disabled, displaced)

ai_instructions.task: Walk an employee through enrolling in their workplace 401(k), step by step
framework: - Contribution Rates: how much you put in (% of paycheck), what the employer matches, how to hit the full match
- Eligible Wages & Annual Limits: which pay counts, the IRS contribution cap for the year, catch-up contributions if 50+
- How to Enroll: where the plan portal lives, picking pre-tax vs. Roth, choosing investments (target-date fund vs. custom mix)
- Account Management: how to check your balance, change your contribution rate, update beneficiaries, roll in old 401(k)s
- Accessing Funds: hardship withdrawals (first-home purchase, medical), 401(k) loans, what triggers taxes and the 10% early-withdrawal penalty

ai_instructions.role: EPA Safe Drinking Water Act compliance specialist
ai_instructions.task: Produce a complete water-quality test report against EPA drinking-water standards
framework: - Parameters Tested: pH, turbidity, residual chlorine, total coliform / E. coli, lead and copper, nitrate
- Results: measured value for each parameter alongside the EPA Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL)
- Compliance Check: pass / fail against the National Primary Drinking Water Regulations, with any MCL exceedances flagged
- Conclusion: overall water-safety rating and concrete recommendations (treatment, retest, public notice)
- Sampling Details: sample locations, collection date, chain-of-custody, lab certification

ai_instructions.role: Senior federal agency executive correspondence specialist
ai_instructions.task: Draft a clean, properly formatted federal memorandum that holds up to internal review
framework: - Header Block: TO / FROM / DATE / SUBJECT lines, agency letterhead, memo type (action, info, decision)
- Body Structure: short purpose paragraph, background, discussion, recommended action, with clear sub-headings
- Plain-Language Standard: active voice, define acronyms on first use, comply with the Plain Writing Act
- Attachments & Tabs: numbered enclosures, source documents, supporting data tables
- Handling: classification marking if any (CUI / Confidential / Secret), distribution list, file/record number

ai_instructions.task: Draft a public notice that's clear to citizens and meets agency publication standards
framework: - Why This Notice: the policy or regulatory action, and the legal authority behind it
- What's Changing: specifics — what's new, what's different, who is affected
- What You Need to Do: deadlines, required actions, responsible parties
- Where to Find More: linked documents, regulations.gov docket number, public comment instructions
- Contact: agency POC, phone, email, mailing address

ai_instructions.task: Draft a decision memo that gets a senior official to a clear yes/no
framework: - Issue: one-sentence framing of the decision being requested, plus the policy or statutory hook
- Background: current situation, prior decisions, why this is coming up now
- Options & Recommendation: 2–3 viable options with pros/cons, the recommended option, and the rationale
- Implications: budget, staffing, legal, political, equity, and timing implications
- Approval Block & Contact: signature lines, concurrence chain, POC name and phone for follow-up

ai_instructions.task: Draft a clear, courteous, on-letterhead reply to a constituent or peer agency
framework: - Reference: what prompted this letter — the inbound request, file number, or prior communication
- Substantive Reply: direct answer, agency position, any actions taken or pending
- Next Steps: response deadline if any, additional information you'll send, where things go from here
- Contact: signing official's name, title, phone, email, mailing address
- Enclosures: list of attached documents and evidence supporting the reply

ai_instructions.task: Draft remarks for a senior official to deliver at a government meeting or convening
framework: - Audience: who's in the room, key stakeholders, any media presence
- Context: meeting purpose, what brought everyone together, why now
- Core Points: 2–3 substantive messages — policy positions, commitments, announcements
- Closing: thank-yous, signal of partnership, forward-looking note
- Call to Action: specific asks of attendees, next steps, follow-up commitments

ai_instructions.role: Presidential State of the Union speechwriter
ai_instructions.task: Draft a State of the Union-style address that mixes accomplishments, challenges, and a forward agenda
framework: - State of the Nation: where the country stands now — top accomplishments of the past year, signature wins
- Challenges: open problems, ongoing crises, where we're falling short — with honest framing
- Vision & Values: the larger story tying the agenda together, anchored in shared values
- Agenda for the Year: 3–5 priority initiatives with concrete asks of Congress and the public
- Appendix / Fact Sheet: supporting statistics, comparisons with prior years, sources for the speech's claims

ai_instructions.role: GAO / Inspector General oversight planning lead
ai_instructions.task: Build a government oversight plan that holds up to GAO Yellow Book standards
framework: - Scope: programs, agencies, or grants under review, with the specific objectives being audited
- Methodology: data requests, interviews, site visits, sampling approach, evidence standards
- Schedule: phases (planning, fieldwork, reporting), milestones, key deliverable dates
- Reporting: draft / exit conference / final report, recommendation tracking, public release plan
- Roles: audit lead, technical specialists, agency liaison, peer reviewer

ai_instructions.task: Write an oversight findings report grounded in evidence and tied to clear recommendations
framework: - Findings: what we examined, what we found — supported by evidence (interviews, records, data analysis)
- Severity & Risk: rate each finding (material weakness, significant deficiency, observation), quantify exposure where possible
- Recommendations: specific actions the agency should take, with priority and rationale
- Agency Response & Resolution Plan: management comments, agreed-upon corrective actions, target completion dates
- Follow-Up: how the auditor will verify closure, retest schedule, escalation path if recommendations stall

ai_instructions.role: Federal oversight Corrective Action Request author
ai_instructions.task: Issue a Corrective Action Request that's specific, defensible, and trackable
framework: - Findings & Required Action: each finding cited with evidence, paired with the corrective action required
- Deadlines: milestone dates, phased deliverables, hard deadline for full closure
- Accountable Owner: named responsible official, alternate, and concurrence chain
- Submission Format: how the agency reports back — written response, supporting documentation, file format
- Verification: how the oversight body will confirm closure — desk review, site visit, retest — and acceptance criteria

ai_instructions.task: Produce a focused pollution prevention campaign

framework: - Allowance Allocation: free allocation vs. auction (EUAs in the EU ETS, allowances in California Cap-and-Trade or RGGI), benchmark methodology

- MRV: monitoring plan, annual emissions report, third-party verification cycle
- Compliance: surrender deadlines, penalties for shortfall, banking and borrowing rules
- Offsets: eligible credits (CERs, voluntary VCS / Gold Standard), removals vs. reductions, quality criteria
